scenario: Connect to Google Drive as migration source
steps:
1. Go to New Migration
2. Select Google Drive as source
3. Click Authorize and complete OAuth
4. Verify connection shows account email
expected_result: Google Drive account connected successfully, user email displayed in source panel

scenario: Connect to OneDrive as migration source
steps:
1. Go to New Migration
2. Select OneDrive as source
3. Complete Microsoft OAuth flow
4. Verify account appears in source
expected_result: OneDrive account connected, tenant info displayed

scenario: Connect to Dropbox as migration source
steps:
1. Select Dropbox as source
2. Authorize via OAuth
3. Verify connection
expected_result: Dropbox account connected successfully

scenario: Connect to Box as migration source
steps:
1. Select Box as source
2. Complete OAuth
3. Verify connection
expected_result: Box account connected and folder tree visible

scenario: Invalid credentials rejected on source connection
steps:
1. Select a source provider
2. Enter invalid/revoked credentials
3. Attempt to connect
expected_result: Clear error message shown, user prompted to retry

scenario: Connect to Google Drive as migration destination
steps:
1. After source connected, select destination
2. Choose Google Drive
3. Authorize different account
4. Verify destination shows correct email
expected_result: Destination Google Drive account connected, distinct from source

scenario: Connect to SharePoint as destination
steps:
1. Select SharePoint as destination
2. Enter tenant URL
3. Authorize
4. Select target site/library
expected_result: SharePoint site connected, document library selectable

scenario: Same account cannot be source and destination
steps:
1. Connect Account A as source
2. Attempt to use same Account A as destination
expected_result: Error shown: source and destination must be different accounts

scenario: Select specific folders for migration
steps:
1. After both connections set up
2. Browse source folder tree
3. Select 3 specific subfolders
4. Confirm selection
expected_result: Only selected folders queued for migration, total file count shown

scenario: Select all folders (full migration)
steps:
1. Click 'Select All' on source folder tree
2. Confirm
expected_result: All folders selected, estimated size displayed

scenario: Deselect individual folders from a full selection
steps:
1. Select All
2. Uncheck 2 specific folders
3. Confirm
expected_result: Excluded folders removed from migration scope, count updated

scenario: Start migration and verify it transitions to Running state
steps:
1. Configure source, destination, folders
2. Click Start Migration
3. Observe status change
expected_result: Migration status changes from Pending → Running within 30 seconds

scenario: Pause and resume a running migration
steps:
1. Start a migration
2. Click Pause when running
3. Verify paused state
4. Click Resume
5. Verify running resumes
expected_result: Migration pauses and resumes without data loss or duplication

scenario: Cancel a running migration
steps:
1. Start migration
2. Click Cancel
3. Confirm cancellation dialog
4. Check status
expected_result: Migration stops, status shows Cancelled, already-migrated files remain at destination

scenario: Migration completes and shows 100% progress
steps:
1. Run a small migration (< 10 files)
2. Wait for completion
3. Check progress bar and status
expected_result: Progress bar reaches 100%, status shows Completed, all files present at destination

scenario: Files migrated maintain original folder structure
steps:
1. Migrate a folder with nested subfolders
2. Check destination after completion
expected_result: Folder hierarchy identical at destination

scenario: Download migration report after completion
steps:
1. Open a completed migration
2. Click Download Report
expected_result: CSV/Excel report downloaded listing all migrated files with status

scenario: Report shows failed files with error reasons
steps:
1. Run migration where some files are expected to fail (locked/unsupported)
2. Download report
expected_result: Failed files listed with specific error message per file

scenario: Create a new backup task for Google Drive
steps:
1. Go to Backup > New Backup
2. Select Google Drive as source
3. Authorize account
4. Choose destination storage
5. Save
expected_result: Backup task created, appears in backup list with Pending status

scenario: Set backup schedule to daily
steps:
1. Create or edit backup task
2. Set schedule to Daily
3. Choose time 02:00 AM
4. Save
expected_result: Schedule saved, next run time shown as next day at 02:00

scenario: Set backup schedule to weekly
steps:
1. Edit backup task
2. Set schedule to Weekly, Monday
3. Save
expected_result: Schedule saved, next run shows next Monday

scenario: Configure backup retention policy
steps:
1. Edit backup task
2. Set retention to 30 days
3. Save
expected_result: Retention policy saved, old backups beyond 30 days will be auto-deleted

scenario: Manually trigger a backup run
steps:
1. Open a backup task
2. Click Run Now
3. Confirm
expected_result: Backup starts immediately, status changes to Running

scenario: Backup completes successfully
steps:
1. Trigger a backup for a small folder
2. Wait for completion
expected_result: Backup status shows Completed, file count and size match source

scenario: Incremental backup only copies changed files
steps:
1. Run initial backup
2. Add 2 new files to source
3. Run backup again
4. Check number of files copied
expected_result: Second run copies only the 2 new files (incremental), not all files

scenario: View backup run history
steps:
1. Open backup task
2. Click History tab
expected_result: List of previous runs shown with date, duration, files count, status

scenario: Filter backup history by status
steps:
1. Open History tab
2. Filter by Failed
expected_result: Only failed runs shown in filtered view

scenario: Browse available restore points for a backup task
steps:
1. Go to Restore
2. Select a backup task
3. View available restore points
expected_result: List of restore points displayed with date/time and file count

scenario: Select a specific restore point by date
steps:
1. Open restore points
2. Pick restore point from 7 days ago
3. Confirm selection
expected_result: Restore point selected, files from that snapshot shown

scenario: Restore all files from a restore point
steps:
1. Select restore point
2. Choose Restore All
3. Select destination
4. Start restore
expected_result: All files from restore point copied to destination, progress shown

scenario: Restore specific files from a restore point
steps:
1. Select restore point
2. Browse files
3. Select 3 specific files
4. Restore to original location
expected_result: Only selected files restored, other files unaffected

scenario: Restore to alternate destination folder
steps:
1. Select files to restore
2. Choose alternate destination folder
3. Start restore
expected_result: Files restored to the alternate folder, original files unchanged

scenario: Conflict resolution: overwrite existing files
steps:
1. Restore files that already exist at destination
2. Choose Overwrite on conflict dialog
expected_result: Existing files overwritten with backup version

scenario: Conflict resolution: keep both files
steps:
1. Restore files that already exist at destination
2. Choose Keep Both on conflict dialog
expected_result: Restored file renamed (e.g. filename (1).ext), both files present

scenario: Verify restored file content matches original
steps:
1. Restore a known file
2. Open restored file
3. Compare content with original
expected_result: File content identical to backup snapshot version

scenario: Migration completion email sent to account owner
steps:
1. Configure notification email in settings
2. Complete a migration
3. Check inbox
expected_result: Email received within 5 minutes of migration completion with summary stats

scenario: Backup failure email notification sent
steps:
1. Configure a backup that will fail (revoke source access)
2. Trigger backup run
3. Check inbox
expected_result: Failure email received with error details and recommended action

scenario: Weekly backup summary email delivered
steps:
1. Enable weekly summary notification
2. Wait for scheduled send (or trigger manually in test env)
3. Check inbox
expected_result: Summary email lists all backup tasks, last run status, next run time

scenario: Disable email notifications for a specific task
steps:
1. Open task settings
2. Toggle notifications OFF
3. Trigger task completion
expected_result: No email sent after task completion

scenario: Add additional notification recipient
steps:
1. Go to task notification settings
2. Add secondary email
3. Complete task
expected_result: Both primary and secondary email addresses receive notifications

scenario: Notification email contains correct task name and stats
steps:
1. Complete a migration named 'Test-Migration-001'
2. Open completion email
expected_result: Email shows correct task name, files migrated count, size, duration

scenario: Email links open correct task in Cloudsfer app
steps:
1. Receive task notification email
2. Click 'View Task' link in email
expected_result: Link opens Cloudsfer app to the specific task detail page

scenario: Register new account with valid email
steps:
1. Go to Cloudsfer registration page
2. Enter valid business email
3. Set password
4. Submit
expected_result: Account created, verification email sent, user redirected to check-email page

scenario: Registration blocked for personal email domains
steps:
1. Attempt registration with gmail.com email
2. Submit
expected_result: Error: only business email addresses accepted

scenario: Duplicate email registration rejected
steps:
1. Register with email that already exists
2. Submit
expected_result: Error: email already registered, link to login or reset password

scenario: Verify account via email link
steps:
1. Register new account
2. Open verification email
3. Click verification link
expected_result: Account activated, user logged in and redirected to dashboard

scenario: Expired verification link shows helpful error
steps:
1. Register
2. Wait for link to expire (24h)
3. Click old link
expected_result: Error shown: link expired, option to resend verification email

scenario: Resend verification email works
steps:
1. Login with unverified account
2. Click Resend Verification
3. Check inbox
expected_result: New verification email received with fresh link

scenario: New user shown onboarding wizard on first login
steps:
1. Complete registration and verify email
2. First login
expected_result: Onboarding wizard shown: step 1 of 3 — choose first migration type

scenario: Onboarding wizard can be skipped
steps:
1. First login triggers wizard
2. Click Skip
expected_result: Wizard closed, user taken to main dashboard

scenario: Download OPA installer for Windows
steps:
1. Go to OPA section
2. Click Download for Windows
3. Save and check file
expected_result: MSI installer downloaded, file size > 5MB, name matches current version

scenario: Install OPA and verify it appears in Cloudsfer portal
steps:
1. Run OPA installer
2. Enter registration token from portal
3. Complete installation
4. Refresh portal OPA page
expected_result: New agent listed in portal with status Online

scenario: Invalid registration token rejected during install
steps:
1. Run installer
2. Enter an invalid/expired token
3. Attempt to complete
expected_result: Installation fails with clear error: invalid or expired token

scenario: Agent shows Online status in portal
steps:
1. Ensure OPA service is running on the machine
2. Open portal OPA page
expected_result: Agent shows Online with last heartbeat within 60 seconds

scenario: Agent shows Offline when service stopped
steps:
1. Stop OPA Windows service
2. Wait 2 minutes
3. Refresh portal
expected_result: Agent status changes to Offline

scenario: Reconnect notification when agent comes back online
steps:
1. Stop agent service (agent goes offline)
2. Restart service
3. Check portal
expected_result: Agent status returns to Online, last seen timestamp updated

scenario: Use OPA to access on-premise file server as source
steps:
1. Create new migration
2. Select File Server (OPA) as source
3. Choose registered agent
4. Enter file share path
expected_result: Folder tree from file server displayed in migration setup

scenario: Migrate from on-premise file server to cloud storage via OPA
steps:
1. Configure migration: OPA file server → Google Drive
2. Select folders
3. Start migration
expected_result: Files transferred from on-premise to cloud, progress shown in portal

scenario: Large file (> 2GB) migrates successfully via OPA
steps:
1. Place a 2.5GB file on file server
2. Include it in migration
3. Start and complete migration
expected_result: Large file migrated intact, file size at destination matches source

scenario: All active tasks visible in task bar
steps:
1. Start 2 migrations and 1 backup simultaneously
2. Open task bar
expected_result: All 3 tasks listed with individual progress indicators

scenario: Completed tasks removed from active task bar
steps:
1. Wait for a task to complete
2. Check task bar
expected_result: Completed task no longer in active list (or moved to completed section)

scenario: Task progress percentage updates in real time
steps:
1. Start a migration
2. Watch task bar for 60 seconds
expected_result: Progress percentage increases without page refresh

scenario: Task bar shows estimated time remaining
steps:
1. Start a large migration
2. Open task bar after 30 seconds
expected_result: ETA displayed next to progress (e.g. '~12 min remaining')

scenario: Pause task from task bar
steps:
1. Open task bar with running migration
2. Click Pause icon
expected_result: Task pauses, icon changes to Play, progress freezes

scenario: Cancel task from task bar
steps:
1. Click Cancel icon on a running task in task bar
2. Confirm dialog
expected_result: Task cancelled, removed from active list

scenario: Click task in task bar navigates to task detail
steps: 1. Click task name in task bar
expected_result: User navigated to full task detail page

scenario: In-app notification shown when task completes
steps:
1. Start a small migration
2. Wait for completion while on a different page
expected_result: Toast/banner notification appears: 'Migration completed successfully'

scenario: Admin can view all registered users
steps:
1. Login as admin
2. Go to Admin panel
3. View Users list
expected_result: All users listed with email, role, status, registration date

scenario: Admin can activate a deactivated user
steps:
1. Find deactivated user in admin panel
2. Click Activate
expected_result: User status changes to Active, user can now login

scenario: Admin can deactivate an active user
steps:
1. Find active user
2. Click Deactivate
3. Confirm
expected_result: User deactivated, cannot login (blocked at auth)

scenario: Admin cannot deactivate own account
steps:
1. Login as admin
2. Open own user detail
3. Attempt to deactivate
expected_result: Deactivate button disabled or error shown: cannot deactivate own account

scenario: Admin can change user role
steps:
1. Open user detail in admin panel
2. Change role from QA to Dev
3. Save
expected_result: Role updated immediately, shown in user list

scenario: Admin can manually verify an unverified user
steps:
1. Find unverified user (no email verification done)
2. Click Verify manually
expected_result: User marked as verified, can now login

scenario: Admin can see all test sessions across all users
steps:
1. Go to Admin > Sessions or User Detail
2. View sessions list
expected_result: All sessions listed with user email, version, environment, date

scenario: Admin can view test results for any user's session
steps:
1. Open a user's session from admin panel
2. View results
expected_result: Full pass/fail/blocked results visible for that session

scenario: Admin panel shows overall system stats
steps: 1. Open admin dashboard
expected_result: KPI cards show: total users, active users, total sessions, pending verifications

scenario: QA user can start a new test session
steps:
1. Login as QA user
2. Go to Start Session
3. Fill version and environment
4. Submit
expected_result: Session created, redirected to test case view with all modules loaded

scenario: Dev user can start a test session
steps:
1. Login as Dev user
2. Start session with Mass Test environment
expected_result: Session created for Dev user, same test case set available

scenario: Session persists across browser tab refresh
steps:
1. Start session and mark 5 test cases
2. Refresh browser tab
expected_result: Same session loaded, all 5 markings preserved

scenario: Mark test case as Pass
steps:
1. Open a test case card
2. Click Pass button
expected_result: Card border turns green, status badge shows Pass, header chip increments

scenario: Mark test case as Fail
steps: 1. Click Fail button on a test case
expected_result: Card border turns red, status badge shows Fail, Fail chip increments

scenario: Mark test case as Blocked
steps: 1. Click Blocked button on a test case
expected_result: Card shows orange Blocked status

scenario: Mark test case as Skip
steps: 1. Click Skip button on a test case
expected_result: Card shows Skip status (grey), counted as tested in progress

scenario: Change status from Pass to Fail
steps:
1. Mark a test as Pass
2. Click Fail on same card
expected_result: Status updated to Fail, Pass chip decrements, Fail chip increments

scenario: Add notes to a test case
steps:
1. Click on notes field of a test case
2. Type 'Reproduced on APP4 only'
3. Wait 1 second
expected_result: Notes auto-saved, persists after page refresh

scenario: Mark All Pass bulk action
steps:
1. Navigate to a submodule with 5 test cases
2. Click Mark All Pass
expected_result: All 5 visible cases marked Pass, progress and chips updated

scenario: Skip All bulk action
steps:
1. Navigate to a submodule
2. Click Skip All
expected_result: All visible cases marked Skip

scenario: Filter test cases by Fail status
steps:
1. Mark some cases as Fail
2. Click Fail filter button
expected_result: Only Fail cards visible, others hidden

scenario: Filter test cases by Pending status
steps: 1. Click Pending filter
expected_result: Only untested cards shown

scenario: Search sidebar modules by keyword
steps: 1. Type 'backup' in sidebar search
expected_result: Only Backup module visible in sidebar, others hidden

scenario: Export session results to Excel
steps:
1. Open an active or completed session
2. Click Export button
expected_result: XLSX file downloaded with Summary sheet + one sheet per module

scenario: Exported Excel contains correct data
steps:
1. Mark 10 test cases across 3 modules
2. Export
3. Open Excel and verify
expected_result: Each marked case appears with correct status, notes, timestamp in relevant sheet

scenario: Dashboard shows all sessions in dropdown
steps:
1. Complete 3 sessions
2. Go to Dashboard
expected_result: Dropdown lists all 3 sessions with tester name, version, date

scenario: Dashboard KPI cards show correct counts
steps:
1. Select a session on dashboard
2. Check Pass/Fail/Blocked/Skip/Pending counts
expected_result: KPI cards match actual result counts from that session

scenario: Module pass-rate chart updates on session change
steps:
1. Select session A on dashboard (view charts)
2. Switch to session B
expected_result: All charts re-render with data from session B

scenario: Activity feed shows recent test results
steps:
1. Mark 5 test cases in the last few minutes
2. Open dashboard
expected_result: Activity feed shows those 5 results with relative timestamps
